mission.title: Install and Configure OVN-Kubernetes on Kubernetes
mission.description: OVN-Kubernetes is an open-source project that provides a robust networking solution for Kubernetes clusters using OVN (Open Virtual Networking) and Open vSwitch at its core. It is a CNI-conformant plugin offering advanced networking features.

steps.title: Clone the OVN-Kubernetes repository
steps.description:
Clone the OVN-Kubernetes repository to access the Helm charts.
```bash
git clone https://github.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes.git
cd ovn-kubernetes
```

steps.title: Install OVN-Kubernetes using Helm
steps.description:
Deploy OVN-Kubernetes as the CNI plugin using the included Helm chart.
```bash
helm install ovn-kubernetes helm/ovn-kubernetes \
  --namespace ovn-kubernetes \
  --create-namespace \
  --set global.image.repository=ghcr.io/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes \
  --set global.image.tag=v1.2.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the OVN-Kubernetes pods are running across the cluster.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace ovn-kubernetes
kubectl get nodes -o wide
```

steps.title: Validate CNI connectivity
steps.description:
Create test pods to verify networking is working correctly.
```bash
kubectl run test-pod-1 --image=busybox --command -- sleep 3600
kubectl run test-pod-2 --image=busybox --command -- sleep 3600
kubectl exec test-pod-1 -- ping -c 3 $(kubectl get pod test-pod-2 -o jsonpath='{.status.podIP}')
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show OVN-Kubernetes pods running on all nodes in the cluster. Pod-to-pod networking should be functional across nodes, and Kubernetes Services should route traffic correctly.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Helm release
uninstall.description: Remove the OVN-Kubernetes Helm release. Note: removing the CNI will disrupt cluster networking.

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and namespace
uninstall.description: Delete OVN-Kubernetes CRDs and the ovn-kubernetes namespace.

upgrade.title: Update the repository
upgrade.description: Pull the latest OVN-Kubernetes source to get updated Helm charts.

upgrade.title: Run helm upgrade
upgrade.description: Upgrade the deployment with the new chart version.

troubleshooting.title: OVN-Kubernetes pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
Check pod logs and ensure OVS (Open vSwitch) is installed on all nodes.
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=ovnkube-node --namespace ovn-kubernetes
```

troubleshooting.title: Pod-to-pod connectivity fails
troubleshooting.description:
Verify OVN northbound and southbound databases are running and connected.
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=ovnkube-db --namespace ovn-kubernetes
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher without a CNI installed (or replacing the existing CNI). Helm, kubectl, and git are required.
